policy.title: RBI Finalizes Climate Risk Disclosure Framework for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI issued final guidelines mandating climate risk disclosures for all SCBs with assets above ₹50,000 crore, effective FY2027-28. Banks must report physical and transition risk exposures using standardized PD adjustment methodologies aligned with NGFS scenarios.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Include Scope 3 Emissions for Top 500 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI amended BRSR Core framework requiring mandatory Scope 3 emissions reporting for the top 500 companies by market capitalization from FY2026-27. Third-party assurance requirements extended to cover climate-related financial metrics.

policy.title: IRDAI Issues Draft Guidelines on Index-Based Parametric Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI released exposure draft permitting insurers to offer parametric products triggered by rainfall, temperature, and flood indices. Guidelines specify minimum correlation thresholds (0.7) between index triggers and actual losses for product approval.

policy.title: Basel Committee Publishes Pillar 1 Climate Risk Capital Add-On Consultation
policy.description: BCBS issued consultative document proposing climate risk capital add-ons under Pillar 1, with differentiated risk weights for high-carbon exposures. Comment period ends July 2026, with implementation targeted for 2028.

policy.title: ISSB Releases Guidance on Emerging Market Implementation of IFRS S2
policy.description: IFRS Foundation published jurisdiction-specific guidance for emerging markets adopting S2, including proportionality provisions for smaller entities and phased Scope 3 timelines. India explicitly referenced as priority adoption market.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: NGFS published updated Phase V scenarios incorporating acute physical risk events at sub-national level, with specific calibration for South Asian monsoon variability and cyclone frequency projections through 2100.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter announced strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to provide portfolio-level physical risk scoring across retail and corporate lending books. Deal reportedly valued at $4M annually, marking Jupiter's first major Indian banking client.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $12M Series A for Supply Chain ESG Platform Expansion
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih closed Series A led by Elevation Capital to expand its supply chain sustainability platform targeting Indian manufacturers and their global buyers. Company claims 200+ enterprise clients across textiles and automotive sectors.

competitors.title: MSCI Launches India-Specific Physical Climate Risk Ratings for 1,500 Companies
competitors.description: MSCI expanded its climate risk ratings to cover 1,500 Indian companies with facility-level physical hazard assessments. Product includes district-level flood, heat stress, and water scarcity scores integrated into existing ESG ratings platform.

competitors.title: Sust Global Releases API for Real-Time Parametric Trigger Monitoring
competitors.description: Sust Global launched Climate Explorer API enabling real-time monitoring of parametric insurance trigger events across 15 perils globally. Product targets insurers and reinsurers designing index-based products for emerging markets.

competitors.title: Watershed Partners with Tata Consultancy Services for Enterprise Carbon Accounting
competitors.description: Watershed announced partnership with TCS to deploy its carbon accounting platform across TCS's Fortune 500 client base, including significant Indian enterprise exposure. Partnership includes joint go-to-market for BRSR compliance solutions.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Signs Agreement with Axis Bank for Agricultural Carbon Credit Origination
competitors.description: CarbonMint partnered with Axis Bank to integrate carbon credit origination into the bank's agricultural lending platform, targeting 500,000 smallholder farmers. Credits will be verified under Verra's new smallholder methodology.

research.title: Climate Risk and Credit: Integrating Physical Hazards into PD Models for Emerging Market Banks
research.description: IFC study analyzing 12 emerging market banks found that incorporating granular physical risk data improved PD model accuracy by 15-22% for agricultural and real estate portfolios. Report recommends minimum 1km resolution hazard data for loan-level risk assessment.

research.title: Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Market Sizing and Product Design Considerations
research.description: Swiss Re estimates South Asian parametric insurance market at $800M by 2030, with India accounting for 60% of potential premium volume. Report identifies monsoon rainfall and cyclone wind speed as most viable trigger indices for agricultural and SME lending protection.

research.title: Physical Risk Transmission Channels to Bank Balance Sheets: Evidence from Indian Flood Events
research.description: RBI working paper found that banks with >15% exposure to flood-prone districts experienced 40-60 bps higher NPAs in the 18 months following major flood events. Study recommends explicit physical risk overlays in ICAAP submissions.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: A Framework for Proxy Estimation
research.description: World Bank report documents that only 23% of listed companies in emerging markets provide climate disclosures meeting ISSB standards. Proposes sector-based estimation methodologies for Scope 1-2 emissions when primary data unavailable.

research.title: NGFS Climate Scenario Analysis: A Practical Guide for Banks in Emerging Markets
research.description: UNEP FI guide provides step-by-step methodology for banks in emerging markets to conduct NGFS-aligned scenario analysis with limited data. Includes case studies from three Indian banks piloting physical risk stress tests.

research.title: Climate Insurance Protection Gap: India Country Assessment
research.description: Munich Re estimates India's climate insurance protection gap at $12.5B annually, with agricultural and MSME sectors most underserved. Report identifies parametric products as most scalable solution for closing gap in next 5 years.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate disclosure framework creates a 18-month runway for StepChange to become the default PD-adjustment infrastructure for Indian banks.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: Indian regulators are moving from consultation to enforcement on climate risk, while global competitors are racing to capture the resulting demand. The window for positioning StepChange as essential infrastructure is narrowing but remains wide open.

The RBI's finalized climate disclosure framework (policy-1) is the most consequential development. By mandating standardized PD adjustment methodologies for banks above ₹50,000 crore, RBI has essentially created a compliance market for exactly what StepChange builds. The explicit alignment with NGFS scenarios—which just released Phase V with enhanced South Asian physical risk granularity (policy-6)—means our hazard data integration approach is now regulatory-endorsed. StepChange should immediately map our PD/LGD models to the RBI's specified methodology and begin outreach to compliance heads at the 40+ banks in scope.

Jupiter Intelligence's HDFC partnership (comp-1) is a direct competitive threat that validates our market thesis. A $4M annual contract for physical risk analytics proves Indian banks will pay for this capability—but it also means a well-capitalized global player is now inside one of India's largest private banks. However, Jupiter's global platform likely lacks the granular Indian hazard data and local regulatory mapping that StepChange can offer. MSCI's India-specific launch (comp-3) adds pressure but their ratings approach is less suited to loan-level PD integration than our modular data products.

The IFC research on PD model improvement (research-1) is ammunition for every sales conversation. A 15-22% accuracy improvement from granular physical risk data is a quantifiable value proposition that risk officers can take to their boards. Pair this with RBI's own working paper showing 40-60 bps NPA impact from flood exposure (research-3), and StepChange has a regulatory-backed, empirically validated case for climate-adjusted credit risk models.

One thing to watch: IRDAI's parametric guidelines (policy-3) with the 0.7 correlation threshold will require precise hazard-trigger alignment—this is a technical moat opportunity if we nail the methodology. One action to take: convene a working session this week to map StepChange's current data products explicitly to RBI's disclosure requirements and identify any gaps before the FY2027-28 compliance clock starts.
